audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for Job's integrity language, sons of God, ha-satan as the Accuser, divine court scene, hedge/hand language, calamity reports, Job's mourning, and refusal to charge the Divine with wrong.

verses.restored: There was a man in the land of Uz whose name was Job. That man was whole, upright, fearing the Divine and turning away from evil.
Seven sons and three daughters were born to him.
His livestock were seven thousand sheep, three thousand camels, five hundred yoke of oxen, five hundred female donkeys, and very many servants. That man was greater than all the sons of the east.
His sons would go and make a feast in the house of each one on his day, and they would send and call their three sisters to eat and drink with them.

When the days of feasting had gone around, Job would send and consecrate them. He would rise early in the morning and offer burnt offerings according to the number of them all, for Job said, Perhaps my children have sinned and blessed/cursed the Divine in their hearts. Thus Job did continually.
Now there was a day when the sons of the Divine came to present themselves before the Eternal, and the Accuser also came among them.
The Eternal said to the Accuser, From where do you come? The Accuser answered the Eternal and said, From roaming through the earth and walking around in it.
The Eternal said to the Accuser, Have you set your heart on my servant Job? For there is none like him on the earth, a whole and upright man, fearing the Divine and turning away from evil.
The Accuser answered the Eternal and said, Does Job fear the Divine for nothing?
Have you not placed a hedge around him, around his house, and around all that belongs to him on every side? You have blessed the work of his hands, and his livestock has burst forth in the land.
But stretch out your hand now and touch all that belongs to him, and surely he will bless/curse you to your face.
The Eternal said to the Accuser, Look, all that belongs to him is in your hand; only against him do not stretch out your hand. So the Accuser went out from the presence of the Eternal.
There was a day when his sons and daughters were eating and drinking wine in the house of their firstborn brother.
A messenger came to Job and said, The oxen were plowing and the female donkeys were feeding beside them,
and the Sabeans fell upon them and took them, and struck the servants with the edge of the sword. I alone escaped to tell you.
While this one was still speaking, another came and said, Fire of the Divine fell from the heavens and burned up the sheep and the servants and consumed them. I alone escaped to tell you.
While this one was still speaking, another came and said, Chaldeans formed three bands, raided the camels, took them, and struck the servants with the edge of the sword. I alone escaped to tell you.
While this one was still speaking, another came and said, Your sons and daughters were eating and drinking wine in the house of their firstborn brother,
and look, a great wind came from across the wilderness and struck the four corners of the house, and it fell upon the young people, and they died. I alone escaped to tell you.
Job rose, tore his robe, shaved his head, fell to the ground, and bowed down.
He said, Naked I came out from my mother’s womb, and naked I shall return there. The Eternal gave, and the Eternal has taken. Let the name of the Eternal be blessed.
In all this, Job did not sin and did not give folly to the Divine.
